_note: JSON sidecar for migration-manifest.yaml. The wizard runtime CLI consumes JSON; YAML is the human-facing companion. Both files MUST stay in sync.

artifact_notes.agents/lib/external_write/operations.py: New external-write operation model — validates each write's values against the operator's allowed set before it leaves the system (native fail-fast on an out-of-vocabulary value).
artifact_notes.agents/lib/external_write/adapters.py: New per-destination write adapters — the only sanctioned path to a real external write; a direct API call outside an adapter is a bypass.
artifact_notes.agents/lib/external_write/broker.py: New approval broker — proposes a write in plain language, records the operator's fresh approval, and writes a per-operation receipt.
artifact_notes.agents/lib/external_write/scan.py: New bypass scanner CLI — flags any external write that goes around the sanctioned adapter path; the build-time review step fails the phase on a violation.
artifact_notes.project_instructions.md: Build-and-operate review step now runs the bypass scanner and fails the phase on a violation; carries the per-agent permission audit table and the controlled-vocabulary write rule.
artifact_notes.operating_discipline.md: Adds the write-integrity discipline — writes use only values from the operator's allowed set, and significant external writes get a fresh plain-language approval.
artifact_notes.quality/rules_library.md: Documents the controlled-vocabulary standing rule and the external-write approval discipline.
artifact_notes.agents/prompts/orchestrator_prompt.md: Orchestrator runs the bypass scanner in the build-time review and is the bound invoker of every external-write surface; fails the phase on a scanner violation.
artifact_notes..claude/receipt_gate.sh: Runtime hook demoted to a backstop behind the build-time scanner and the broker's fresh-approval check.

migrations.migration_steps: 1. Run the update check (it reports what's new + the recommendation). 2. On the operator's yes, the assistant applies it ("${WIZARD_HOME:-$HOME/agent-wizard}/scripts/wizard" self-upgrade --to v0.8.0 --apply). 3. A backup is taken first; the operator's own data, rules, credentials, and logs are never touched.
migrations.preflight: Verify the operator project carries a .wizard/manifest.json whose foundation_bundle_version is v0.7.0 (or any version in the supported range). A backup is taken automatically.
migrations.rollback: Restore from .wizard/backups/pre-v0.8.0/ and set foundation_bundle_version back to v0.7.0.
migrations.notes: Minor-additive release to v0.8.0: adds the external-write integrity feature — a checked write substrate (operations, adapters, an approval broker, and a build-time bypass scanner) emitted only into systems that write back to an external destination, plus per-agent permitted-write derivation and a build-time scanner gate wired into the operating-layer prose. No breaking changes; all prior operator project files remain valid.
